title: AI Recruitment Screening Tool
description: An AI system that automatically screens job applications, ranks candidates based on resume analysis, and filters out applicants before human review. Uses NLP to parse CVs and ML models to predict candidate suitability.
notes: Falls under EU AI Act Annex III, Category 4 (Employment)

title: Facial Recognition in Public Spaces
description: A real-time facial recognition system deployed in public areas for general surveillance purposes by law enforcement, continuously scanning and identifying individuals without specific warrants or targeted searches.
notes: Likely prohibited under EU AI Act Article 5(1)(h)

title: Credit Scoring AI
description: An AI system used by banks to evaluate the creditworthiness of loan applicants. Analyzes financial history, spending patterns, and demographic data to generate credit scores and lending decisions.
notes: EU AI Act Annex III, Category 5 (credit scoring)

title: Medical Diagnosis Support Chatbot
description: An AI chatbot that helps patients describe symptoms and provides preliminary diagnostic suggestions. It collects health data and uses NLP to match symptoms with potential conditions, then recommends whether to see a doctor.
notes: Healthcare AI with sensitive health data

title: Content Recommendation Engine
description: A machine learning system that recommends articles, videos, and products to users based on their browsing history and engagement patterns. Used on a media platform with millions of users.
notes: Transparency obligations for recommender systems

title: Exam Proctoring AI
description: An AI system used in educational institutions to monitor students during online exams. Uses webcam analysis, eye tracking, and keystroke dynamics to detect potential cheating behavior and flags suspicious activity.
notes: EU AI Act Annex III, Category 3 (Education)

title: Insurance Risk Pricing Model
description: An AI system that calculates insurance premiums based on individual risk profiles. Analyzes personal health data, lifestyle factors, location data, and historical claims to determine pricing for health and life insurance.
notes: EU AI Act Annex III, Category 5 (insurance risk assessment)

title: Customer Support Chatbot
description: A conversational AI chatbot deployed on a retail website to answer frequently asked questions, help with order tracking, and assist with returns. Does not make decisions about customer eligibility or access to services.
notes: Transparency obligation: must inform users they are interacting with AI

title: Employee Productivity Monitoring AI
description: An AI system that tracks employee computer activity, monitors keystrokes, captures screenshots, and measures productivity metrics. Generates performance scores used in promotion and termination decisions.
notes: EU AI Act Annex III, Category 4 (monitoring workers)

title: Biometric Attendance System
description: An AI-powered biometric system using fingerprint and facial recognition to track employee attendance and working hours. Data is stored locally and used only for attendance management.
notes: Biometric data processing requires DPIA under GDPR

title: Social Credit Scoring System
description: A government-operated AI system that assigns social scores to citizens based on their behavior, social connections, and online activity. The score affects access to public services, transportation, and benefits.
notes: Explicitly prohibited under EU AI Act Article 5(1)(c)

title: Predictive Policing System
description: An AI system that predicts crime hotspots and identifies individuals likely to commit crimes based on historical data, neighborhood demographics, and behavioral profiling.
notes: Prohibited under EU AI Act Article 5(1)(d) when based solely on profiling

title: AI-Powered Spam Filter
description: An email spam detection system that uses machine learning to classify incoming emails as spam or legitimate. Operates on email metadata and content patterns without accessing personal data beyond email addresses.
notes: Minimal risk, no significant compliance concerns

title: Autonomous Vehicle Navigation System
description: An AI system that controls autonomous vehicle navigation, making real-time driving decisions including lane changes, speed adjustment, obstacle avoidance, and emergency braking.
notes: Safety component of regulated product, critical infrastructure

title: AI-Driven Welfare Benefits Eligibility
description: A government AI system that evaluates applications for social welfare benefits, housing assistance, and unemployment insurance. Makes preliminary eligibility determinations based on submitted documentation.
notes: EU AI Act Annex III, Category 5 (public services)

title: Deepfake Video Generation Tool
description: An AI tool that generates realistic synthetic video content by swapping faces and altering voices. Available as a consumer application without restrictions on use.
notes: Transparency obligation for synthetic content under EU AI Act

title: Border Control Automated Screening
description: An AI system used at border crossings to screen travelers, analyze travel documents, perform risk assessments of incoming individuals, and flag persons of interest for additional inspection.
notes: EU AI Act Annex III, Category 7 (migration and border control)

title: Smart Inventory Management System
description: An AI system that optimizes warehouse inventory by predicting demand, automating reorder points, and managing stock levels. Operates on product and sales data without any personal information.
notes: No personal data, no impact on individuals

title: AI Judicial Sentencing Advisor
description: An AI system that provides judges with sentencing recommendations based on case details, precedent analysis, and risk of recidivism assessments. Used in criminal courts to inform sentencing decisions.
notes: EU AI Act Annex III, Category 8 (administration of justice)

title: Emotion Recognition in Classroom
description: An AI system deployed in classrooms that monitors students' facial expressions and body language to assess engagement levels and emotional states. Data is used by teachers to adjust teaching methods.
notes: Prohibited under EU AI Act Article 5(1)(f) - emotion recognition in education
